config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus jadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, kekuasaan mudah disalahgunakan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan menindasnya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should have priority in some cases, but I do not accept the idea that it should generally override individual rights. From my perspective in China, people often value social stability, collective responsibility, and public order, and those concerns can be very real, especially in times of crisis or rapid development. But I also think that if individual rights are treated as disposable, it becomes too easy for authority to justify almost anything in the name of the common good. A healthy society needs a real balance: strong enough collective action to protect the public, but clear limits that prevent the individual from being sacrificed whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Yang saya anggap benar adalah kepentingan publik boleh dibatasi sementara dalam keadaan sangat mendesak, tetapi hak dasar seperti kebebasan, keamanan, dan martabat tidak boleh dianggap bisa dikorbankan seenaknya. Justru masyarakat yang sehat, termasuk di Indonesia, dibangun dari individu yang dilindungi haknya, bukan dari individu yang terus diminta mengalah tanpa batas. Kalau keseimbangan itu hilang, yang muncul bukan ketertiban, melainkan ketakutan dan ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in my view society’s interests should not automatically outrank individual rights. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people emphasize order, coordination, and the public good, especially in a large society where instability can hurt everyone, but I also know how easily “for the common good” can become a blanket excuse for overreach. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then the burden always falls on ordinary people to sacrifice, while power faces too few limits. For me, the right position is a strict balance: society can ask for temporary restraint in real emergencies, but it should never be allowed to erase basic rights whenever convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil dan menutup penyalahgunaan wewenang. Memang ada keadaan darurat yang menuntut pembatasan sementara, tetapi itu harus sangat ketat, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar seperti kebebasan, keamanan, dan martabat. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua secara umum, yang terjadi bukan solidaritas, melainkan kebiasaan membiarkan kekuasaan terlalu leluasa. Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat justru paling kuat kalau hak tiap orang dilindungi dengan serius.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because in my view society’s interests cannot be allowed to override individual rights by default. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the cultural emphasis on order, collective responsibility, and social stability, but I also know how dangerous it becomes when “for the common good” turns into a blank check for coercion. The other side is right that emergencies may justify temporary limits, yet those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable. A society is healthier when it protects rights first and asks for sacrifice only when it is truly necessary, not whenever it is convenient for power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
